fix_1_stateless_sessions.change: All cron agent calls now use --session-id with a date-based unique ID (e.g. morning-growth-20260326). Each cron run gets a completely fresh session with no prior conversation history loaded.
fix_1_stateless_sessions.mechanism: SESSION_ID variable set per run from $(date +%Y%m%d) — guaranteed unique per day, never reuses growing history

fix_2_consolidated_crons.before: 7 agent cron calls/day: 2x morning (8am), 2x afternoon (5pm), 3x evening (8pm, 8:15pm, 8:30pm)
fix_2_consolidated_crons.note: Afternoon pipeline checks (5pm x2) removed entirely — saves 2 agent calls/day. Morning split into 8:00 + 8:05 to avoid race condition between growth and starter sessions.

fix_3_large_files_archived.files_accessible: All archived files remain at workspace-reporter/archive/ and can be loaded explicitly when needed

projected_impact.stateless_sessions: Eliminates history replay entirely — the single biggest cost driver. Each cron call now costs only system_prompt + task_message tokens, not system_prompt + full_growing_history.
projected_impact.cron_consolidation: 2 fewer agent API calls per day
projected_impact.reporter_context_reduction: 60% reduction in reporter context size per call
projected_impact.estimated_cost_reduction: 70-85% reduction in cache write tokens from scheduled cron jobs

remaining_cost_drivers: Interactive Telegram conversations with agents still replay session history — this is expected and acceptable
The afternoon pipeline check has been removed — if you want it back, re-add as a stateless call
